summary: Advance Signal Pro: Mobile app (Android/iOS) for automated financial chart analysis using a local candle analysis engine. The results screen shows: a small thumbnail of the original screenshot, a prominent probability sentence (e.g., '70% de alta / 30% de baixa'), and a detailed quality analysis for the next candle direction. Analysis is slower/more deliberate for better UX. Features: AI-powered chart analysis with support/resistance detection, last two chart patterns identified, trend direction (ALTA/BAIXA), precision level (baixa/média/alta), volume indicator (baixo/alto), and auto-detected timeframe from user settings (M1/M5/M10). Dark/light themes, Portuguese (Brazil) interface, chart upload (camera/gallery/clipboard/drag-drop), analysis history, daily operation management (3/4/6/8 ops per day with midnight reset), operation confirmation buttons (green=followed, red=not followed).
architectural_notes: Mobile-first architecture for Android and iOS platforms
Modular, clean, and scalable code structure
Image processing pipeline supporting PNG, JPG, JPEG, PDF formats
Professional trading-style UI with dark/light theme support
Fast, lightweight performance optimized for small screens
Navigation flow: Dashboard → Upload → Processing → Results → History/Settings
Fast processing target: <3 seconds for analysis results
API system removed: no external Gemini/REST API calls. All chart analysis is performed locally using a candle analysis engine based on image processing and pattern rules.
Results screen must auto-read selected timeframe (M1/M5/M10) from user settings and display it alongside the analysis — no manual input required from the user.
known_issues: Analysis error: 'Erro ao processar análise. Tente novamente.' occurring when users attempt to analyze charts — local candle analysis engine failing to process uploaded image.

Save result error: 'Não foi possível salvar o resultado' appears after successful local analysis — backend actor save call failing (timeout or incompatibility), causing 'ERRO NA ANALISE' to display instead of navigating to results.
Timeframe display bug: Results screen shows M1 as the period even when user selected M5 or M10 in Settings — timeframe is not being read correctly from user configuration.
Settings page: timeframe selector not persisting/propagating selected value (M1/M3/M5); daily operation limit selector not displaying or functioning correctly with normal numbers.

Timeframe selector in Settings not updating the active value when user selects M3 or M5 — the displayed/active timeframe does not change after selection, persisting the old value instead of the newly chosen one.

existing_features.title: Create a Dashboard home screen with four primary action buttons: 'Analyze Chart', 'Analysis History', 'Settings', and an AI status indicator showing system readiness. Use a minimalist black, white, and gray color scheme with large, prominent buttons and simple iconography in a professional trading app style.

Implement chart upload functionality supporting multiple input methods: camera capture, gallery selection, clipboard paste, and drag-and-drop. Accept PNG, JPG, JPEG, and PDF file formats. Display a file selection interface with clear options for each upload method.

Create an AI processing animation screen that displays four sequential loading stages: 'Loading image', 'Processing data', 'Detecting patterns', and 'Generating result'. Show animated progress indicators for each stage to provide visual feedback during chart analysis.

Create a Results screen that displays the complete analysis output: BUY or SELL signal, confidence percentage, current trend, precision level (Low/Medium/High), suggested timeframe, detailed explanatory text, visual markings drawn on the chart image (lines and arrows), a 'Save Analysis' button, and a 'Share' button. Design with clear visual hierarchy and professional trading app aesthetics.

Implement an Analysis History screen that lists all previous analyses with date/time stamps and results. Allow users to tap any historical entry to reopen and view the full analysis details. Display entries in reverse chronological order with clear visual separation between items.

Create a Settings screen with configuration options for: theme toggle (dark/light mode), AI sensitivity adjustment, default timeframe preferences, signal notifications toggle, and language selection. Each setting should have clear labels and intuitive controls (toggles, sliders, dropdowns as appropriate).

Design and implement a cohesive minimalist visual theme using black, white, and gray colors throughout the application. Create a professional trading interface aesthetic with dark mode as default and optional light mode. Ensure large touch-friendly buttons, simple clear icons, highly visible charts, and premium appearance with clean layouts and ample whitespace.

Rewrite the Gemini API prompt in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to be a minimal, direct instruction focused solely on predicting the next candle direction. The new prompt must: (1) tell Gemini to look at the image and find the candlestick chart area, ignoring everything else on the screen; (2) examine the last 5 visible candles and the overall trend; (3) predict whether the NEXT candle is more likely to be bullish or bearish; (4) return ONLY a valid JSON object with these fields: sinal ('COMPRA' if next candle likely bullish, 'VENDA' if likely bearish, 'SEM ENTRADA' if unclear), tendencia ('ALTA'/'BAIXA'/'LATERAL'), confianca (number 0-100), padroes (array of short Portuguese pattern name strings, max 3 items), explicacao (one sentence in Portuguese explaining the prediction). Update the Gemini API response parsing logic in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to robustly extract JSON from the Gemini response text. Gemini sometimes wraps JSON in markdown code fences (```json ... ```) or adds surrounding text. The parser must: strip any markdown code fence wrappers before JSON.parse; attempt to extract the first {...} block from the response text if direct parsing fails; log the raw response text to the console for debugging; throw a descriptive AnalysisApiError with Portuguese message 'Resposta inválida da IA — tente novamente' if no valid JSON can be extracted.

Update the image-to-base64 conversion step in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to ensure the image is always sent as a clean base64 string. Remove the base64 data URI prefix (e.g., 'data:image/jpeg;base64,') before placing the value in the inlineData.data field — Gemini Vision API requires raw base64 data without the data URI prefix. Also ensure the mimeType field is correctly set to the actual file MIME type (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'image/png', 'image/webp') and never hardcoded to a fixed value.

Create a local candle analysis engine in frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts that analyzes an uploaded chart image client-side without any external API calls. The engine must: (1) accept a File or Blob image input; (2) draw the image onto an HTML Canvas to read pixel data; (3) detect the candlestick chart region by scanning for alternating green/red vertical pixel columns against a dark or light background; (4) identify the last 5–7 visible candles by detecting their body color (green = bullish, red = bearish), body height, wick length, and relative position; (5) compute a next-candle probability score based on: trend direction across the detected candles (ascending tops/bottoms = ALTA, descending = BAIXA, otherwise LATERAL), last candle pattern (engulfing, hammer, shooting star, doji, pin bar detected from body-to-wick ratio and color), momentum (consecutive same-color candles increase confidence); (6) return a typed LocalAnalysisResult object containing: sinal ('COMPRA' | 'VENDA' | 'SEM ENTRADA'), tendencia ('ALTA' | 'BAIXA' | 'LATERAL'), confianca (number 0–100), probabilidade_alta (number 0–100), probabilidade_baixa (number 0–100), padroes (string[] in Portuguese), forca ('fraca' | 'média' | 'forte'), explicacao (one Portuguese sentence summarizing the prediction), and erro (optional string if chart region cannot be detected). The probabilities must sum to 100. If the chart region cannot be reliably detected, set erro to 'Não foi possível identificar o gráfico corretamente' and return a SEM ENTRADA signal.

The screen must have a dark background and display the following sections top to bottom: (1) A header row with back arrow, title 'Resultado da Análise', and subtitle 'Análise técnica de candles'; (2) Two side-by-side dark tiles — left tile shows a clock icon, 'TEMPO' label, the countdown timer in large bold white (e.g., '00:36'), and 'restante' subtitle; right tile shows a clock icon, 'ENTRAR ÀS' label, the entry time in large bold cyan/teal (e.g., '20:32'), and 'horário de entrada' subtitle; (3) A large full-width green banner for COMPRA (showing '↗ COMPRA' in large bold white text) or red banner for VENDA (showing '↙ VENDA'), with 'Tendência: ALTA • Confiança: 95%' on one line and a 'Força: forte' badge centered below it; (4) A 2×2 grid of dark tiles: top-left shows 'Tendência' label with a colored arrow icon and 'ALTA' value in green (or 'BAIXA' in red), top-right shows 'Precisão' label with a target icon and the precision percentage/value in cyan, bottom-left shows 'Volume' label with a bar chart icon and the volume value (e.g., 'Baixo') in purple, bottom-right shows 'Time Frame' label with a clock icon, the timeframe value (e.g., 'M1') in amber/orange bold, and a subtitle (e.g., '1 min/vela'); (5) Below the grid, display the uploaded chart image as a full-width (or near full-width) thumbnail with support/resistance overlay lines drawn on it. All text must be in Portuguese (Brazil).

Update the chart thumbnail display in Results.tsx so the uploaded screenshot is shown as a large, full-width (or near full-width) image below the 2×2 info grid — matching the reference design in image-13.png where the chart occupies the lower portion of the screen. Remove any previous small corner thumbnail placement. Retrieve the image data URL from sessionStorage under the 'chartImage' key and render it with the ChartOverlay component so support (green dashed) and resistance (red dashed) lines are drawn on top.

Update the Tendência tile in the 2×2 grid of Results.tsx to display a colored upward/downward arrow icon inline with the ALTA or BAIXA label — green arrow for ALTA, red arrow for BAIXA — matching the visual style in image-13.png. Update the Precisão tile to display its value (percentage or Baixa/Média/Alta label) in cyan/teal text. Update the Volume tile to display its value in purple text. Update the Time Frame tile to display the timeframe value (M1/M3/M5) in amber/orange bold text with a subtitle line below it (e.g., '1 min/vela' for M1, '3 min/vela' for M3, '5 min/vela' for M5).

Update the signal banner in Results.tsx to display inline within the banner: the signal arrow and label ('↗ COMPRA' or '↙ VENDA') in large bold white text, a middle line showing 'Tendência: [value] • Confiança: [XX]%', and a third line showing a centered pill badge 'Força: [forte/média/fraca]' — all within the green (COMPRA) or red (VENDA) full-width banner. This matches the layout visible in image-13.png. The banner must span the full width of the screen with generous vertical padding.

Fix the localStorage key mismatch between frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx and frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts. Audit both files to confirm they use the exact same key string when writing and reading the timeframe value. If the keys differ, unify them to a single constant (e.g., 'settings_timeframe'). The Settings page must write the selected value ('M1', 'M3', or 'M5') to localStorage under this key immediately on selection, and localCandleAnalysis.ts must read from this exact same key at analysis execution time — never at module load time.

Fix the Settings page timeframe selector in frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx so that selecting M3 or M5 immediately updates the displayed/active selected state in the UI, persists the value to localStorage under the unified key (matching localCandleAnalysis.ts), and saves to the backend via the updateSettings mutation. The selector must not visually revert to its previous value after selection. On page reload, the selector must restore the last saved value. Show the Portuguese success toast 'Configuração salva!' after each successful save.

Fix the countdown timer and Time Frame tile in frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to use the timeframe value from the analysis result's 'timeframe' field. The timer initial duration must be: M1 = 60 seconds, M3 = 180 seconds, M5 = 300 seconds. The 'Entrar às HH:MM' entry time must be computed as the current time at page load plus the correct duration. The Time Frame tile in the 2×2 info grid must display the value from the analysis result's timeframe field (e.g., 'M5'), never hardcoding or defaulting to M1 when another timeframe was selected. Also update frontend/src/hooks/useCountdownTimer.ts to accept the timeframe-derived duration correctly.

Redesign the AnalyzeChart page (frontend/src/pages/AnalyzeChart.tsx) to support uploading up to 3 chart screenshots simultaneously — one per timeframe slot: M1, M3, and M5. Replace the current single-image upload area with three distinct upload slots arranged vertically (or in a card list). Each slot must be clearly labeled with its timeframe badge (e.g., 'M1 — 1 minuto', 'M3 — 3 minutos', 'M5 — 5 minutos'). Each slot must independently support file picker, camera capture, clipboard paste, and drag-and-drop input. Each slot shows a preview thumbnail once an image is selected, with an 'X' remove button to clear that slot. At least one slot must be filled before the 'Analisar Gráfico' button becomes active. The 'Analisar Gráfico' button at the bottom remains as the single call-to-action to start analysis. All labels must be in Portuguese (Brazil). Maintain the existing dark-theme professional appearance consistent with the current UI shown in image-14.png.

Update the sessionStorage persistence logic in frontend/src/pages/AnalyzeChart.tsx so that when the user proceeds to analysis, all uploaded chart images are stored in sessionStorage under separate keys: 'chartImage_M1', 'chartImage_M3', and 'chartImage_M5' (each containing a base64 data URL, or null/absent if not provided). Also store a 'chartImages_count' key indicating how many slots were filled (1, 2, or 3). Clear any previously stored single 'chartImage' key. If storing to sessionStorage fails (e.g., quota exceeded), show a Portuguese error toast 'Erro ao preparar imagem. Tente novamente.' and do not navigate.

Update frontend/src/pages/ProcessingScreen.tsx to handle the new multi-image session storage format. On mount, read all available chart images from sessionStorage ('chartImage_M1', 'chartImage_M3', 'chartImage_M5'). For each image present, run the local candle analysis engine (analyzeChartImage) sequentially. Display the four animated processing stages as usual. After all analyses complete, merge the results into a combined multi-timeframe result object stored in sessionStorage under the key 'analysisResult', then navigate to the Results page. If only one image was uploaded, the behavior must remain identical to the previous single-image flow. All error handling (missing image, analysis failure, chart not identifiable) must apply per image slot and surface a clear Portuguese error message if any slot fails.

Update the LocalAnalysisResult TypeScript interface in frontend/src/types/analysisTypes.ts and the AnalysisResult interface in frontend/src/utils/mapApiResponse.ts to support an optional 'multiTimeframe' field — an array of objects, each containing: timeframe ('M1' | 'M3' | 'M5'), and all standard analysis fields (sinal, tendencia, confianca, probabilidade_alta, probabilidade_baixa, padroes, forca, explicacao, precisao, volume, suportes, resistencias). This allows the Results screen to display a per-timeframe breakdown when multiple images were analyzed.

Update frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to display a multi-timeframe analysis summary section when the 'multiTimeframe' field is present in the analysis result and contains more than one entry. This section must appear below the main signal banner as a horizontal scrollable row of compact timeframe cards (one per analyzed timeframe: M1, M3, M5). Each card displays the timeframe label, signal (COMPRA ↑ / VENDA ↓ / SEM ENTRADA), trend (ALTA/BAIXA/LATERAL), and confidence percentage for that specific timeframe. The card for the user's currently selected default timeframe is visually highlighted. If only one image was analyzed, this multi-timeframe section is hidden and the existing single-result layout is shown unchanged. All labels must be in Portuguese (Brazil).

Update the TimeframeUploadSlot component (frontend/src/components/TimeframeUploadSlot.tsx) and the AnalyzeChart page (frontend/src/pages/AnalyzeChart.tsx) so that each individual upload slot rectangle (M1, M3, M5) becomes clipboard-paste-active when the user clicks or taps on it. When a slot is in the active/focused state, pressing Ctrl+V (or triggering a paste event on mobile) must paste the clipboard image directly into that specific slot only — not into all slots simultaneously. Only one slot can be the active paste target at a time. The active slot must display a visible focus indicator (e.g., a highlighted border or subtle glow) so the user knows which slot will receive the pasted image. Clicking outside any slot or selecting a file/camera image for a different slot deactivates the previous active slot. This replaces any existing global paste listener that pastes into the first available slot regardless of user intent.

new_feature_requests.summary: Dashboard with Analyze Chart, History, Settings buttons and AI status indicator
Chart upload via camera, gallery, clipboard paste, and drag-drop (PNG/JPG/JPEG/PDF)
AI processing animation showing loading stages (image load, data processing, pattern detection, result generation)
Results screen showing BUY/SELL signal, confidence %, trend, precision level, timeframe suggestion, explanation text, visual markings on chart, save and share buttons
Analysis history with date/time, results, and ability to reopen past analyses
Settings for theme toggle (dark/light), AI sensitivity, default timeframes, signal notifications, and language selection
Dark/light minimalist theme with black, white, and gray color scheme, trading-style UI, large buttons, simple icons, visible charts, premium appearance
App icon with minimalist 'ASP' letters and display name 'Advance Signal Pro'
Complete Portuguese (Brazil) localization for all UI elements, buttons, titles, messages, and notifications
Restrict timeframe selection to M1 (1 minute - default), M5 (5 minutes), and M10 (10 minutes) only
Optimize analysis for Pocket Option charts with fast scalping detection (<3 seconds processing time)

Implement external AI analysis via REST API: POST /analisar-grafico with multipart/form-data image upload, receiving JSON response with signal (COMPRA/VENDA), trend, confidence, detected patterns, and explanatory text
Prepare API integration layer to support OpenAI ChatGPT Vision, Google Gemini Vision, or custom Python backend
Separate frontend from AI logic: remove local analysis implementation and use external API responses only

Update the backend to remove or deprecate local analysis storage if it conflicts with the new external API flow. Ensure that analysis results returned by the external API can still be saved to the backend's history (backend/main.mo) by adapting the storage functions to accept externally-generated analysis data.
Update app tagline to 'Análise de Gráficos com IA & Sinais de Trading 1.0'

Simplify Gemini API analysis to basic chart analysis: support/resistance levels, last candle patterns, and trend direction. Accept full mobile phone screen screenshots and analyze only the chart area within the image, ignoring all other screen elements (notifications, status bar, broker UI).

Advanced candle analysis: calculate probability of next candle being bullish or bearish (%), suggest action based on previous candle high/low breakout, provide numeric entry/stop-loss/target example, and display disclaimer
Operation history with green/red confirmation buttons per operation (green=followed, red=not followed)

Daily operation management system: user selects daily operation count (3, 4, 6, or 8), progressive clock tracking each operation, resets at midnight, shows progress (e.g., '2/3 operações completadas hoje')

Update the Gemini API prompt in frontend/src/services/analysisApi.ts to include advanced candle analysis instructions. The prompt must instruct the AI to analyze the last 3–5 visible candles on the chart and return two additional fields in the JSON response: 'probabilidade_alta' (number 0–100: probability that the next candle will be bullish) and 'probabilidade_baixa' (number 0–100: probability that the next candle will be bearish). The probabilities must sum to 100. Additionally, the prompt must request: 'acao_sugerida' (string: 'COMPRA', 'VENDA', or 'AGUARDAR') based on whether the current candle breaks above the previous candle's high (buy) or below the previous candle's low (sell), and 'exemplo_operacao' (object with fields: 'entrada' number, 'stop' number, 'alvo' number) providing a numeric example using the previous candle's high/low as reference prices. The prompt must also consider volume, trend direction across the last candles, and simple patterns (engulfing, hammer, doji) when calculating probabilities.

Extend the TypeScript interface ApiAnalysisResponse in frontend/src/types/apiTypes.ts to include the new fields returned by the advanced candle analysis prompt: 'probabilidade_alta' (optional number), 'probabilidade_baixa' (optional number), 'acao_sugerida' (optional string: 'COMPRA' | 'VENDA' | 'AGUARDAR'), and 'exemplo_operacao' (optional object with fields 'entrada': number, 'stop': number, 'alvo': number). Add JSDoc comments explaining each field.

Update frontend/src/utils/mapApiResponse.ts to map the new API response fields ('probabilidade_alta', 'probabilidade_baixa', 'acao_sugerida', 'exemplo_operacao') into the internal AnalysisResult format. Preserve these values so they are available for display in the Results screen.

Update frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to display the advanced candle analysis output. Add a new section below the existing signal card showing: (1) 'Probabilidade de alta: XX%' and 'Probabilidade de baixa: XX%' with visual percentage bars; (2) 'Ação sugerida: [COMPRA/VENDA/AGUARDAR]' with color coding (green for COMPRA, red for VENDA, yellow for AGUARDAR); (3) 'Exemplo de operação' block showing 'Entrada: [value]', 'Stop: [value]', 'Alvo: [value]'; (4) A disclaimer text in Portuguese: '⚠️ Este é um cálculo de probabilidade baseado em análise técnica, não garante resultados.' All labels must be in Portuguese (Brazil). Only render this section if the probability or operation example fields are present in the result.

Update the backend actor in backend/main.mo to extend the stored analysis record type to include optional fields for the advanced candle analysis: probabilidadeAlta (optional Float or Nat), probabilidadeBaixa (optional Float or Nat), acaoSugerida (optional Text), entradaExemplo (optional Float), stopExemplo (optional Float), alvoExemplo (optional Float), and operationFollowed (optional Bool, representing whether the user confirmed they followed the suggested operation). Update the storeAnalysis function to accept and persist these new optional fields. Ensure backward compatibility with existing stored analyses that lack these fields.

Update the backend actor in backend/main.mo to add a daily operation management system. Add a per-user settings field for 'dailyOperationLimit' (Nat, accepting values 3, 4, 6, or 8, defaulting to 3). Add a function 'getDailyOperationProgress(userId)' that counts how many analyses have been stored for the current UTC day (after 00:00) and returns the count along with the user's configured daily limit. Add a function 'setDailyOperationLimit(limit: Nat)' to update the user's daily limit. Store analyses with a timestamp so day-based filtering is possible.

Update frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to add React Query hooks for the new daily operation management backend functions: a 'useGetDailyProgress' hook that calls 'getDailyOperationProgress' and returns today's operation count and limit, a 'useSetDailyOperationLimit' mutation hook that calls 'setDailyOperationLimit', and a 'useUpdateOperationFollowed' mutation hook that calls 'updateOperationFollowed' with an analysis ID and boolean value.

Update frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to add two confirmation buttons per analysis result: a green button labeled 'Segui a operação ✓' and a red button labeled 'Não segui ✗'. When clicked, each button calls the 'useUpdateOperationFollowed' mutation with the current analysis ID and true (green) or false (red). After confirmation, show a brief Portuguese feedback message (e.g., 'Operação registrada!'). Disable both buttons after one is selected. Display the daily progress indicator (e.g., '2/3 operações completadas hoje') using data from 'useGetDailyProgress'.

Update frontend/src/pages/History.tsx and frontend/src/components/HistoryListItem.tsx to display the green/red confirmation status for each past operation. If 'operationFollowed' is true, show a green indicator labeled 'Seguiu'; if false, show a red indicator labeled 'Não seguiu'; if null/undefined, show no indicator. Also display the daily progress summary at the top of the History page: 'X/Y operações completadas hoje' using data from 'useGetDailyProgress'.

Update frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx to add a 'Daily Operation Limit' setting. Display a selector labeled 'Operações por dia' with four options: 3, 4, 6, and 8. Use the existing SettingRow component for layout. On change, call the 'useSetDailyOperationLimit' mutation hook and show a Portuguese success toast. Load the current value from the backend settings using the existing useSettings hook pattern. Show the current daily progress below the selector: 'Hoje: X/Y operações realizadas'.

Update frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx to display a daily operation progress bar or text badge in the AI status area. Show the text 'X/Y operações hoje' using data from 'useGetDailyProgress'. Use a subtle progress bar styled consistently with the dark/light theme. This should update in real time when the user returns to the dashboard after completing an analysis.

Update image analysis system: auto-detect and crop only the candle chart area from screenshots (ignore all broker UI), apply contrast/binarize colors to highlight candles, analyze using price action rules only (no broker text/values), return error if chart cannot be isolated, display thumbnail of original screenshot in corner of results layout. Output: Tendência, Padrões, Força (fraca/média/forte), Confiança %, Sinal (COMPRA/VENDA/SEM ENTRADA) in Portuguese.

Fix analysis not working on screenshots: simplify Gemini API prompt to perform basic next-candle analysis without changing the app interface. Focus on predicting next candle direction based on visible chart data.

Remove the entire external Gemini/API system (API key config, API calls, API settings field, endpoint config). Replace with a local candle analysis engine that analyzes the uploaded chart image and predicts next candle direction.

Results screen: display a small thumbnail of the original uploaded screenshot beside the analysis results, show a prominent sentence like '70% de probabilidade de ALTA / 30% de BAIXA na próxima vela', and display a quality next-candle analysis based on visible candle patterns (trend, last candle shape, momentum).

Remove backend Gemini API key storage from backend/main.mo. Delete the getGeminiApiKey and setGeminiApiKey functions and any associated stable storage for the API key. Ensure backward compatibility so existing analyses and other settings stored in the backend are unaffected.

Results screen: minimize the uploaded chart photo (thumbnail), redesign layout to show logo above 'Analisando gráfico' text, with clean analysis info layout. Accept any photo upload type (screenshot, copy/paste, file). Animate loading bars with 1%-100% percentage counter beside each bar; bars styled in black and white with white glow/shine effect. Professional app appearance.

Update frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx to display the uploaded chart image as a minimized thumbnail. The thumbnail must be small (approximately 80×60px), positioned in a corner of the results layout (top-right or bottom-right), and must not dominate the results view. Retrieve the image data URL from sessionStorage using the 'chartImage' key. Style the thumbnail with a subtle border and rounded corners consistent with the active dark/light theme.
Slow down the processing/loading animation stages for a more deliberate, premium feel (each stage should take longer to advance)

Detailed next-candle analysis: after loading the chart screenshot, detect which candle the chart is currently on and which timeframe (M1/M5/M10) the user selected in settings — auto-read from configuration. Display analysis explaining what action to take for the next candle entry based on the detected candle and selected timeframe.
AI-powered chart analysis: detect support and resistance levels, identify the last two chart patterns visible in the chart, and display them in the results after the loading completes.

Results screen: show directional guidance section with trend direction (ALTA/BAIXA), precision level (Baixa/Média/Alta), and volume indicator (Baixo/Alto) — all in Portuguese. Auto-detect and display the timeframe from the user's settings configuration.

Audit backend/main.mo to ensure the storeAnalysis function (or equivalent) does not reject calls from anonymous principals or return hard errors that cause the frontend to treat the save as a fatal failure. If the function currently requires authentication and the caller may be anonymous, add a graceful fallback: either allow anonymous saves with a generated ID or return a success variant even when the caller is not authenticated, so the frontend never receives an unhandled error from a save attempt.

Fix timeframe display in Results: the 'Período de Tempo' shown in results must reflect the timeframe actually selected by the user in Settings (M1/M5/M10), not default to M1. Read the selected timeframe from settings/localStorage and use it as both the countdown timer duration and the displayed timeframe value.

Make countdown timer duration match selected timeframe: M1=1 minute operation, M5=5 minutes operation, M10=10 minutes operation. The timer on the results screen must count down the full duration of the selected timeframe candle.
Improve the next-candle analysis explanation text (below 'Padrões Detectados') to be more professional and detailed — use proper technical trading language in Portuguese Brazil.

Add a recommendation phrase below the probability bars (ALTA/BAIXA) indicating whether conditions are favorable to trade or not — e.g., 'Boa oportunidade para operar' or 'Aguarde melhor momento' in Portuguese.

Add WIN/LOSS feedback buttons at the bottom of the Results screen (when scrolled down). Green 'WIN' button for profit and red 'LOSS' button for loss. Record the outcome in the analysis history so each past operation shows WIN or LOSS status.

Fix Settings page timeframe selector to work correctly when M1, M3, or M5 is selected (add M3 as new option replacing M10), persist the selection, and ensure it propagates to analysis results. Fix daily operation limit selector to display and function with normal numbers (3, 4, 6, 8).

Update the backend actor in backend/main.mo to accept 'M3' as a valid timeframe value in the user settings record and in stored analysis records. Ensure existing stored data with M1, M5, or M10 remains backward compatible and is not corrupted.

Audit frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to verify the setDailyOperationLimit mutation and getDailyOperationProgress query are correctly wired to the backend actor. Confirm the Motoko function signatures in backend/main.mo match the frontend call signatures exactly (parameter types, return types). Fix any mismatches that cause the daily limit save or progress fetch to fail silently or return incorrect values.

Fix the timeframe propagation in frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts to ensure the localStorage key used to read the timeframe is exactly identical to the key used by Settings.tsx to write it. The timeframe must be read at analysis execution time (not at module load time). If the stored value is 'M1', 'M3', or 'M5', it must be stored verbatim in the LocalAnalysisResult's timeframe field without any transformation or default override.

Fix the Results screen (frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx) so that the 'Time Frame' tile in the 2×2 info grid and the countdown timer duration both use the timeframe value from the analysis result's timeframe field. The timer duration must be: M1 = 60 seconds, M3 = 180 seconds, M5 = 300 seconds. The 'Entrar às HH:MM' entry time must be computed as the current time at page load plus the correct duration for the selected timeframe. The Time Frame tile must display 'M5' when the user selected M5, never defaulting to M1.

Update AnalyzeChart page to allow uploading up to 3 chart screenshots at once (one per timeframe: M1, M3, M5). Each upload slot is labeled with its timeframe. The local candle analysis engine analyzes all provided charts and combines/summarizes the results for a multi-timeframe analysis view on the Results screen.

Update AnalyzeChart page: each timeframe upload slot (M1, M3, M5) must support clipboard paste (Ctrl+V) when the user clicks/focuses on that specific slot rectangle — clicking a slot activates it so Ctrl+V pastes the image into that slot only.
